target.name: Astera Labs
target.founder: Jitendra Mohan, Sanjay Gajendra, Casey Morrison

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $23.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Cerebras
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $23B

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.5B

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Astera Labs is a public-stage semiconductor company that has raised over $100M. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables: Cerebras ($23.0B), Credo Semiconductor ($5.0B), Ouster ($1.5B), and Aeva Technologies ($1.0B).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. The four public semiconductors peers yield a median valuation of $3.2B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $23.0B. This wide dispersion reflects genuine heterogeneity in the sector—scale, profitability, and addressable market vary materially across these companies. A price-to-sales anchoring method using broader public semiconductor multiples (25.3x median across ALAB, NVDA, AMD) cannot be applied because Astera Labs' revenue is not disclosed in available data.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.8B to $3.7B, centered on the peer median of $3.2B. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single applicable method and a modest peer sample. Key uncertainties include Astera's revenue trajectory and profitability profile relative to peers, which would sharpen any revenue multiple approach, and whether the four comparables adequately represent the company's true competitive position within semiconductors. The wide range among peers ($1.0B–$23.0B) also signals that stage and sector alone do not determine valuation; finer-grained operational metrics are needed to narrow confidence.
